pattern: Setting every free edge to its minimum legal weight gives the shortest possible distance, so nudging one free edge at a time toward the gap closes it without ever overshooting.
hints: Set every -1 edge to weight 1 first and run Dijkstra -- that gives the smallest distance the graph can ever have, since 1 is the cheapest legal weight.
If that smallest distance already exceeds the target, no assignment can work; if it already equals the target, you're done, and the interesting case is when it's still too small.

Walk the -1 edges one at a time: recompute the current shortest distance, and if it's still short of the target, raise just that edge's weight by exactly the remaining gap, then re-check -- a single edge's raise can never overshoot the target by more than it needed to.

edge_inputs.description: several free edges, one needs stretching to reach the target
impossible: the only free edge cannot influence the target pair
the fixed-and-free mix already hits the target at baseline
smallest possible graph, one free edge
no free edges at all, distance already matches target
no free edges at all, distance can never change to match target
a fixed alternate path caps the shortest distance no matter how the free edges are set
target at the maximum legal value

stress.description: random connected graphs (spanning tree plus a few extra edges) with mixed fixed/free weights
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 30))
    nodes = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(nodes)
    edges = []
    edge_set = set()
    for i in range(1, n):
        a = nodes[i]
        b = nodes[rng.randint(0, i - 1)]
        u, v = min(a, b), max(a, b)
        w = -1 if rng.random() < 0.6 else rng.randint(1, 10 ** 7)
        edges.append([u, v, w])
        edge_set.add((u, v))
    extra = rng.randint(0, min(5, n))
    attempts = 0
    while extra > 0 and attempts < 50:
        attempts += 1
        a, b = rng.randint(0, n - 1), rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        if a == b:
            continue
        u, v = min(a, b), max(a, b)
        if (u, v) in edge_set:
            continue
        edge_set.add((u, v))
        w = -1 if rng.random() < 0.4 else rng.randint(1, 10 ** 7)
        edges.append([u, v, w])
        extra -= 1
    source = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    destination = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    while destination == source:
        destination = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    target = rng.randint(1, 10 ** 7)
    return [n, edges, source, destination, target]
